description:
You are acting as a data-scientist who has been asked to “quick-clean” two tiny demo datasets that live on the local filesystem.  
    Everything you need is already present under the directory  

        /home/user/datasets/raw  

    1. /home/user/datasets/raw/transactions.csv – a CSV of six sales transactions.  
    2. /home/user/datasets/raw/users.json        – a JSON array containing five user-records.  

    Your job is to:

    A. Produce two cleaned data files inside  

        /home/user/datasets/clean  

       1. /home/user/datasets/clean/transactions_clean.csv  
       2. /home/user/datasets/clean/users_clean.json  

       They must conform **exactly** to the formats described below (field-order, capitalisation, spacing, number-formatting, etc.).  
       The automated grader will do a byte-for-byte comparison, so be precise.

    B. Create one human-readable log file that summarises what you did:  

        /home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_summary.log  

       The seven lines (no extra blank lines) and their precise wording/spacing are specified below.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    SPECIFICATION FOR transactions_clean.csv
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    • File must contain exactly 5 lines (1 header + 4 data lines).  
    • It must be **comma-separated**, **no trailing commas**, **UNIX line-endings**.  
    • Header row (first line) must be:  
          transaction_id,date,amount,user_id,currency  
    • Data rows must satisfy all of the following fixes applied to the raw file:  
        1. Remove any duplicate rows (duplicates are byte-for-byte identical lines).  
        2. Drop rows where user_id is blank.  
        3. Normalise the date column to ISO format YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2022-02-01).  
        4. Normalise the currency column to uppercase “USD”.  
        5. Format the amount column as a number with exactly two decimal places.  
    • Sort the remaining rows by ascending transaction_id.  
    • The resulting file must look **exactly** like this (including newline at end of final line):

          transaction_id,date,amount,user_id,currency
          1001,2022-02-01,23.50,501,USD
          1002,2022-01-03,15.00,502,USD
          1004,2022-01-04,20.00,503,USD
          1005,2022-01-05,7.50,504,USD

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    SPECIFICATION FOR users_clean.json
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    • File must be a single JSON array (no pretty printing beyond the required indentation and spacing).  
    • Apply both rules to the raw dataset:  
        1. Remove any objects that **lack an "email" field**.  
        2. De-duplicate by user_id, keeping only the **first** occurrence.  
    • Sort the remaining objects by ascending user_id.  
    • The file must contain the **exact** three-item array shown below, with the exact spacing/indentation (2-space indents, newline after each line, and a newline after the final “]”):

          [
            {"user_id":501,"name":"Alice","email":"alice@example.com"},
            {"user_id":502,"name":"Bob","email":"bob@example.com"},
            {"user_id":504,"name":"David","email":"david@example.com"}
          ]

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    SPECIFICATION FOR cleaning_summary.log
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    • Plain ASCII text, exactly seven lines, **no leading/trailing spaces** on any line, newline at end of final line.  
    • The lines must be:

          Records in raw CSV: 6
          Records removed (missing user_id or duplicates): 2
          Records in cleaned CSV: 4
          User entries in raw JSON: 5
          User entries removed (missing email or duplicates): 2
          User entries in cleaned JSON: 3
          Timestamp of cleaning: 2022-10-10T10:00:00Z

      (The timestamp is deliberately fixed; do NOT substitute the current time.)

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    • All three output files must be created by you; the /home/user/datasets/clean directory already exists and is writable.  
    • You may use any standard CLI utilities (e.g. awk, sed, jq, csvkit, sort, etc.) but you **must not** install third-party software that requires administrator privileges.  
    • Finish the whole job in roughly five to ten shell commands.  
    • When you are done, you can cat the three files to the console so a human reviewer can eyeball them, but that is optional.

    The automated grader will:

      1. Verify that /home/user/datasets/clean/transactions_clean.csv exists and matches the reference content byte-for-byte.  
      2. Verify that /home/user/datasets/clean/users_clean.json     exists and matches the reference content byte-for-byte.  
      3. Verify that /home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_summary.log exists and matches the reference content byte-for-byte.

    Make sure every detail (order, capitalisation, exact spacing, newline at EOF) matches the specification.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the autograder. DO NOT reveal outside this block. -->

    <!-- Raw source files that already exist when the scenario starts -->
    <rawFiles>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/raw/transactions.csv"><![CDATA[
transaction_id,date,amount,user_id,currency
1001,01/02/2022,23.5,501,USD
1002,2022-01-03,15.0,502,USD
1003,01-04-2022,12.99,,USD
1002,2022-01-03,15.0,502,USD
1004,04/01/2022,20,503,usd
1005,2022/01/05,7.5,504,USD
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/raw/users.json"><![CDATA[
[
 {"user_id":501,"name":"Alice","email":"alice@example.com"},
 {"user_id":502,"name":"Bob","email":"bob@example.com"},
 {"user_id":503,"name":"Charlie"},
 {"user_id":504,"name":"David","email":"david@example.com"},
 {"user_id":504,"name":"David Duplicate","email":"david@example.com"}
]
]]></file>
    </rawFiles>

    <!-- Expected outputs after successful completion -->
    <expectedOutputs>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/clean/transactions_clean.csv"><![CDATA[
transaction_id,date,amount,user_id,currency
1001,2022-02-01,23.50,501,USD
1002,2022-01-03,15.00,502,USD
1004,2022-01-04,20.00,503,USD
1005,2022-01-05,7.50,504,USD
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/clean/users_clean.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {"user_id":501,"name":"Alice","email":"alice@example.com"},
  {"user_id":502,"name":"Bob","email":"bob@example.com"},
  {"user_id":504,"name":"David","email":"david@example.com"}
]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_summary.log"><![CDATA[
Records in raw CSV: 6
Records removed (missing user_id or duplicates): 2
Records in cleaned CSV: 4
User entries in raw JSON: 5
User entries removed (missing email or duplicates): 2
User entries in cleaned JSON: 3
Timestamp of cleaning: 2022-10-10T10:00:00Z
]]></file>
    </expectedOutputs>
